sizing_rule.note: Adjust position sizing by optimal f level. Full f maximizes geometric growth but increases variability; fractional f (e.g., half f = 0.50 multiplier) reduces variability at cost of slower compounding. Position size varies with f fraction employed; N trades to goal = ln(goal)/ln(geometric_mean).

source_rule_text: Position sizing should be adjusted based on the optimal f level, where higher f values increase both profit rate and variability, while fractional f (e.g., half f) reduces variability at the cost of slower geometric growth.
